conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. 

I have three children - Alex, who is 7, Blake, who is 12, and Charlie, who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10).

Now I need to ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for each child.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific circumstances where you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Could you please tell me: **Which country are you currently living in?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living in Spain may affect your eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you might still qualify.

Could you please tell me: **Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces), or are you working for an organisation that is treated as a Crown servant?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or working for any organisation like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. 

Could you please tell me: **Do you or any member of your family have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Based on what you've told me, I need to understand a bit more about your situation. Could you please tell me: **Are you or your children currently receiving any benefits or payments from the UK, or do you have any other connection to the UK benefits system (for example, are you planning to return to the UK, or do your children live in the UK while you're in Spain)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My children all live with me here in Spain. We're not currently receiving any UK benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me make sure I have a complete picture of your situation.

One final question: **Are you or have you been working in the UK or paying UK National Insurance contributions while living in Spain?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not working in the UK and I'm not paying UK National Insurance contributions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children.

Based on the official UK government guidance, I need to provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children** - Alex, Blake, or Charlie.

**The reason:** The main requirement for Child Benefit is that you normally need to live in the UK. While there are exceptions for people living abroad, these apply to specific circumstances such as:
- Being a Crown servant (diplomat, member of HM Forces, etc.)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Working in the UK or paying UK National Insurance contributions

Unfortunately, you don't meet any of these exceptions. Since you're living in Spain with your children and don't have the qualifying connections to the UK, you don't meet the residency requirements for claiming Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK with your children - you would then be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex (age 7) - To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad apply to Crown servants or those living in certain countries under specific circumstances (such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside). Since you are living in Spain, are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and are not working in or paying UK National Insurance, you do not meet the residency requirements for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 12) - To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad apply to Crown servants or those living in certain countries under specific circumstances (such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside). Since you are living in Spain, are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and are not working in or paying UK National Insurance, you do not meet the residency requirements for claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie (age 10) - To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad apply to Crown servants or those living in certain countries under specific circumstances (such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside). Since you are living in Spain, are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and are not working in or paying UK National Insurance, you do not meet the residency requirements for claiming Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children (Alex age 7, Blake age 12, and Charlie age 10) and is currently living in Spain with all three children. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. While there are exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants or those with settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme), the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions. The claimant is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not working in the UK or paying UK National Insurance contributions. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit and is not eligible for any of the three children.
